title: Examples, Delimiters, Templates, and Structured Output · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Stabilize a classification and extraction prompt across varied inputs. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a tested prompt template with diverse examples after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose relevant and diverse examples that cover boundaries without teaching accidental shortcuts.
Include counterexamples and near misses when category boundaries are material.
Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

stimulus.title: Template Versioning case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: prompt examples structure. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Prompts are informal text and do not need change control.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Version fixed prompt text separately from variable inputs and evaluation data. Artifact under review: a tested prompt template with diverse examples.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Template Versioning, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Template Versioning applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Version fixed prompt text separately from variable inputs and evaluation data.
